notes: Each eval drives the axon4to5-migrate skill with the event-store recipe in single mode. Coverage: Spring Boot + JPA (Success, explicit @Bean + @EntityScan required), Spring Boot + Axon Server (Success, explicit @Bean AggregateBasedAxonServerEventStorageEngine), native + Axon Server (Success, registerEventStorageEngine on EventSourcingConfigurer), and Mongo blocker (Blocker B1). Fixtures live in evals/fixtures/synthetic/.

evals.name: spring-jpa-explicit-bean
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `1: spring-jpa-explicit-bean` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/70-event-store/event-store-jpa.md`. Reach for `examples/event-store/01-spring-boot-jpa.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** EventStoreConfigSpringJpa.java is an AF4 Spring Boot configuration class with `EmbeddedEventStore` + `JpaEventStorageEngine`. Apply the event-store recipe (JPA backend, Spring Boot path): (1) delete the `EmbeddedEventStore` @Bean and the `EventStorageEngine storageEngine()` @Bean; (2) add a new `@Bean EventStorageEngine eventStorageEngine(EntityManagerFactory, EventConverter)` returning `new AggregateBasedJpaEventStorageEngine(new JpaTransactionalExecutorProvider(entityManagerFactory), eventConverter, UnaryOperator.identity())`; (3) add AF5 imports; (4) also add `@EntityScan(basePackages = {"com.example", "org.axonframework", "io.axoniq.framework"})` as a comment NOTE since there is no main application class in the fixture — note in Result that caller must add @EntityScan to their @SpringBootApplication class.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-event-store` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: EventStoreConfigSpringJpa.java rewritten: EmbeddedEventStore deleted, explicit @Bean AggregateBasedJpaEventStorageEngine added with JpaTransactionalExecutorProvider + EventConverter; @EntityScan note in Result; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-event-store

evals.name: spring-axon-server-explicit-bean
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `2: spring-axon-server-explicit-bean` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/70-event-store/event-store-jpa.md`. Reach for `examples/event-store/02-spring-boot-axon-server.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** EventStoreConfigSpringAxonServer.java is an AF4 Spring Boot @Configuration class with an `@Bean AxonServerEventStore`. Apply the event-store recipe (Axon Server backend, Spring Boot path): (1) delete the AF4 `@Bean AxonServerEventStore eventStore(...)` method; (2) add `@Bean EventStorageEngine storageEngine(AxonServerConnectionManager connectionManager, EventConverter eventConverter)` returning `new AggregateBasedAxonServerEventStorageEngine(connectionManager.getConnection(), eventConverter)`; (3) add AF5 imports (`io.axoniq.framework.axonserver.connector.api.AxonServerConnectionManager`, `io.axoniq.framework.axonserver.connector.event.AggregateBasedAxonServerEventStorageEngine`, `org.axonframework.eventsourcing.eventstore.EventStorageEngine`, `org.axonframework.messaging.eventhandling.conversion.EventConverter`); (4) remove AF4 imports. No @EntityScan needed for Axon Server backend.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-event-store` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: EventStoreConfigSpringAxonServer.java: AF4 AxonServerEventStore @Bean deleted; explicit @Bean AggregateBasedAxonServerEventStorageEngine added; AF5 imports present; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-event-store

evals.name: native-axon-server-configurer
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `3: native-axon-server-configurer` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=native`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/70-event-store/event-store-jpa.md`. Reach for `examples/event-store/03-native-axon-server.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** EventStoreConfigNativeAxonServer.java is an AF4 native (non-Spring) configuration class using `DefaultConfigurer.defaultConfiguration()` + `configurer.configureEventStore(config -> AxonServerEventStore.builder()...)`. Apply the event-store recipe (Axon Server backend, native path): (1) replace `DefaultConfigurer.defaultConfiguration()` with `EventSourcingConfigurer.create()`; (2) replace `configurer.configureEventStore(...)` with `configurer.registerEventStorageEngine(config -> { AxonServerConnectionManager manager = config.getComponent(AxonServerConnectionManager.class); return new AggregateBasedAxonServerEventStorageEngine(manager.getConnection(), config.getComponent(EventConverter.class)); })`; (3) replace `buildConfiguration()` with `build()` and change return type to `AxonConfiguration`; (4) add AF5 imports; (5) remove AF4 imports.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-event-store` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: EventStoreConfigNativeAxonServer.java: DefaultConfigurer replaced with EventSourcingConfigurer; registerEventStorageEngine with AggregateBasedAxonServerEventStorageEngine; buildConfiguration() replaced with build(); AF5 imports; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-event-store

evals.name: spring-mongo-blocker
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `4: spring-mongo-blocker` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/70-event-store/event-store-jpa.md`. Reach for `examples/event-store/01-spring-boot-jpa.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** EventStoreConfigMongoBlocker.java is an AF4 Spring Boot configuration with `MongoEventStorageEngine`. Detect blocker B1 (MongoEventStorageEngine — no AF5 release of axon-mongo). Source MUST stay byte-identical — no AggregateBasedJpaEventStorageEngine, no AggregateBasedAxonServerEventStorageEngine added. Emit Blocker B1 with Options (move-to-axon-server / move-to-jpa / pause-migration / skip / revert / solve-manually).

This eval expects a **Blocker** outcome. The fixture contains an AF4 shape that has no AF5 equivalent (e.g. `@DeadlineHandler`, `SagaTestFixture`, `snapshotTriggerDefinition`, Map-typed `@AggregateMember`, `MongoEventStorageEngine`, the `@MessageHandlerInterceptor` method-annotation form). Comment out the blocking code, add a `// TODO: no AF5 equivalent` note, and surface the blocker in the Result block.

Do NOT edit the source file beyond what SKILL.md Step 2 prescribes for blockers (comment out + `// TODO: no AF5 equivalent`). Write the `**Result:**` block to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md` with `**Result:** 🚧 Blocker`, `**Recipe:** axon4to5-event-store`, a `**Notes:**` line naming the blocker, and an `**Options:**` list enumerating `skip`, `revert`, `solve-manually`.
evals.expected_output: MongoEventStorageEngine detected; source untouched; Result: 🚧 Blocker B1.

assertions.text: No AF5 JPA engine added (source untouched)
No AF5 Axon Server engine added (source untouched)

evals.name: spring-axoniq-serializer-to-converter
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `5: spring-axoniq-serializer-to-converter` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The pattern this eval exercises is: `patterns/10-dependencies/serializer-to-converter.md`.

**Eval-specific guidance:** `SerializerConfig.java` is an AF4 Spring `@Configuration` class exposing a Jackson-backed `Serializer` `@Bean`. Apply the serializer-to-converter pattern: (1) rewrite the import `org.axonframework.serialization.Serializer` → `org.axonframework.conversion.Converter`; (2) rewrite the import `org.axonframework.serialization.json.JacksonSerializer` → `org.axonframework.conversion.json.JacksonConverter`; (3) change the `@Bean` method return type to `Converter` and rewrite `JacksonSerializer.builder()` to `JacksonConverter.builder()`; (4) rename the bean method `serializer` → `converter` (conventional, matches the new SPI); (5) update any internal references (parameter types, helper methods) so no AF4 `Serializer` type remains. Do NOT migrate the `Configurer` import — that is out of scope for this eval (treat its presence as a secondary concern; if you remove the helper method, that is fine, but the assertions only check the serializer/converter SPI rename).

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-event-store` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: SerializerConfig.java rewritten: Serializer SPI renamed to Converter under org.axonframework.conversion; JacksonSerializer.builder() → JacksonConverter.builder(); imports updated; bean method renamed to converter(); Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-event-store
